title: age-group — field classification
description: AgeGroup carries commercial + safeguarding reference data. No PII on the row itself (Athletes reference it, but the AgeGroup row never carries an Athlete's DOB). The safeguarding-critical fields are `min_age_inclusive` + `max_age_inclusive` + `is_youth` — mutations to these can invalidate downstream Athlete eligibility for their currently-enrolled Teams. Bounds + cutoff changes are financial-adjacent (invoice pricing sometimes ties to age-tier tuition rates) and audit-critical for child-safety compliance (COPPA + FERPA + GDPR-child).

regulated_minor: AgeGroup.is_youth (drives minor-safeguarding rules for every downstream Team + Athlete row)
AgeGroup.min_age_inclusive (mutations may invalidate Athlete eligibility; audit-critical)
regulated_financial: AgeGroup.is_default (per-tuple default drives tier-pricing defaults in Finance module Wave 4)
audit_log: AgeGroup.min_age_inclusive (every change written to `audits`; downstream Athlete eligibility depends on it — audit-critical for compliance replays)
AgeGroup.sport_key (mutation reshapes the sport-specific override chain; audit-warn)
AgeGroup.is_default (per-tuple default flip; downstream tier-pricing consequences)
AgeGroup.deleted_at (archive + restore + hard-delete all written; the audit row outlives the AgeGroup for compliance replay)

notes: AgeGroup rows themselves carry NO PII. Athletes reference AgeGroups; the Athlete row carries the DOB; the AgeGroup row carries only the min/max bounds and cutoff rule. This separation is deliberate — the AgeGroup catalogue is tenant reference data that can be cached indefinitely per-tenant, while Athlete PII stays scoped by tenant + branch + guardian permission.

`is_youth` is a DENORMALISED boolean set by the observer from `max_age_inclusive < 18`. It's stored on the row so downstream safeguarding filters (which permissions to require, which parental-consent banners to render, which retention window to apply to logs referencing this AgeGroup) index cleanly instead of re-parsing the bounds on every read.

`is_default` and `is_seeded` are two distinct flags. `is_default=true` is one per scoping tuple (tenant, organization, sport_key) and marks the tenant's preferred/fallback group. `is_seeded=true` marks rows created by the platform's default catalog seeder — those cannot be deleted (only edited / archived), which distinguishes them from tenant customs.

Compliance retention on audit rows: 7 years for minor-safeguarding financial records (matches Finance retention). Even after an AgeGroup is hard-deleted, its bounds-change audit rows survive so a subsequent COPPA / FERPA / GDPR-child compliance replay can reconstruct which Athletes qualified for which groups at any past point in time.

Cross-referenced sport-specific overrides: when `sport_key IS NOT NULL`, the row represents an override for that sport (e.g. `sport_key='football', name='U12'` uses Jan 1 cutoff; `sport_key='swimming', name='U12'` uses birthdate exact). Multiple AgeGroups with the same `name` but different `sport_key` values are the normal shape — they answer the same eligibility question different ways per sport.
